svg.aria-label: 4.4 SM_120 TensorCore Internal Architecture Overview — execution units, RF/SMEM connections, pipeline structure, and precision format routing

g.text: SM_120 SMSP TensorCore Unit: Execution, Connectivity & Pipeline

svg.text: 1 TC unit per SMSP × 4 SMSP per SM = 4 concurrent MMA slots per SM (saturates at 4 warps)

g.text: TENSOR CORE EXECUTION UNIT (1 per SMSP) · pipe_tensor
Reads 10 source regs (A[4] + B[2] + C[4]) from RF per MMA dispatch · ~16 cyc read time sets throughput floor
Selects subpipe based on opcode encoding · Cross-format (e4m3↔e5m2): 0 cyc · Cross-family (OMMA↔QMMA): +0.94 cyc (6%)
NCU: subpipe_hmma · Pipeline depth: 1.81 · Latency: 29 cyc · Throughput: 15.88 cyc/mma
All formats: 29 cyc latency · 15.88 cyc/mma throughput (K-independent, format-agnostic)
Block-scale overhead: 0 cyc · Sparse metadata: 0 cyc · Cross-format mix: 0 cyc
NCU: subpipe_imma · Depth: 4.09 · Lat: 27 cyc · Tput: 6.55 cyc/mma
2.42× faster than HMMA · 8-bit MUL units (4× smaller area)
Separate FP64 execution unit · cuBLAS prefers DFMA path (0.96 TFLOPS)
D accumulator writeback → RF (4 regs × 4B × 32 threads = 512 B/MMA) · reuse C=D for K-loop
TC operates on independent port within SMSP
PRECISION FORMAT SCALING · SAME PIPELINE, DIFFERENT K WIDTH
Systolic array width ≥ K=64 elements. Narrower formats underutilize array; K is a FLOP-density knob, not a pipeline-depth knob.
Source: [S9/case_1-8] latency/ILP · [S9/case_27] RF→TC BW · [S9/case_5] reg pressure · [S9/case_35-57] format sweep · [S9/case_101] IMMA
